description: Per-species signature features for part-level grading and prompt-injection. Each feature carries (a) a human-readable label for the grading checklist, (b) a prompt clause that can be injected into the SUBJECT block to lock the feature in at render time, and (c) optional anti_negative clauses that go into the DO NOT block. When a feature shows up as 'missing' in N user grades, the boost composer can promote its severity weight + add its prompt_clause to the next retry.

tiger.label: Stripe pattern present
tiger.prompt_clause: with bold black tiger-stripe pattern across the body and tail rendered as folk-painted stripes, not as photographic fur

elephant.label: Trunk shape + curl
elephant.prompt_clause: with a long curled trunk clearly visible and rendered as a folk-painted curve, not a stub

elephant.label: Tusks visible (if male) OR clean tuskless face

rhino.label: Single horn on nose
rhino.prompt_clause: with one prominent horn on the nose (Greater One-horned Rhinoceros — NEVER two horns)

rhino.label: Armor-plate skin folds at shoulders + hips

sloth-bear.label: White V chest blaze
sloth-bear.prompt_clause: with the distinctive WHITE V-shaped or U-shaped chest blaze prominently visible on the chest fur as a clean folk-white panel contrasting against the surrounding fur — this is the species-identifying feature and MUST be preserved
sloth-bear.anti_negative: no missing chest blaze, no all-black chest, no all-brown chest

sloth-bear.label: BLACK body fur (not brown)
sloth-bear.prompt_clause: with shaggy BLACK fur (sloth bear is NOT a brown bear, NOT a grizzly)
sloth-bear.anti_negative: no brown bear coloring, no grizzly coloring, no honey/tan body

sambar-deer.label: Three-tined branched antlers (male)
sambar-deer.prompt_clause: with large three-tined branched antlers if male, OR a clean antlerless head if female — never stubs or single spikes

nilgiri-tahr.label: Grizzled dark fur with silver saddle

gaur.label: Heavy boss horns
gaur.prompt_clause: with massive curved boss horns (heavy curved horns meeting in a bony shield/boss across the forehead) — the gaur's defining feature, not deer antlers and not cattle stubs

gaur.label: White socks on lower legs
gaur.prompt_clause: with distinctive WHITE STOCKINGS on the lower legs from knee to hoof — clean cream-white panels visibly contrasting against the dark body

gaur.label: Prominent shoulder hump
gaur.prompt_clause: with a prominent powerful muscle hump over the shoulders rising clearly above the back-line — the gaur's iconic bull silhouette

gaur.label: Dark coffee-black body
gaur.prompt_clause: with a deeply dark coffee-black body fill above the white stockings — this contrast between dark body and white legs is non-negotiable

indian-leopard.label: Rosette spot pattern
indian-leopard.prompt_clause: with the iconic leopard rosette spots covering body and tail — clusters of small dark folk-medallions on the golden-tawny base, the species' signature

indian-leopard.label: Long thick tail
indian-leopard.prompt_clause: with a long thick tail (nearly the body's length) curving distinctively

peacock.label: Full massive fanned plumage (not stubby)
peacock.prompt_clause: with a MASSIVE FULLY-FANNED tail plumage spread wide behind the bird — the train must be the dominant visual element of the composition, occupying at least 60% of the frame width, never stubby or partial or folded-down — this is THE iconic peacock display and the rendered image is incomplete if the train is small, partial, or absent
peacock.anti_negative: no stubby tail, no folded plumage, no small train, no missing fan

peacock.label: Full peacock color palette (not monochrome)
peacock.prompt_clause: with the FULL multi-color iridescent peacock palette across the plumage — vivid emerald-green + sapphire-blue + gold + vermillion + bronze all visible as distinct folk-color zones in the train feathers — NEVER monochrome, NEVER a single dominant body color, the train must read as a riot of saturated folk colors
peacock.anti_negative: no monochrome plumage, no faded colors, no single-color train, no missing colors

peacock.label: Tail-eye-spots (ocelli) in plumage
peacock.prompt_clause: with the iconic peacock tail-eye-spots (ocelli) clearly painted as concentric folk-medallions distributed across the fanned train

striped-hyena.label: Vertical body stripes
striped-hyena.prompt_clause: with bold VERTICAL black stripes across the body (NOT spots, NOT horizontal stripes) — the striped hyena's signature pattern rendered as folk-painted bands

striped-hyena.label: Erectile back mane
striped-hyena.prompt_clause: with a prominent erectile back mane/crest of stiff hair running from neck to mid-back

striped-hyena.label: Sloped back (front taller than rear)
striped-hyena.prompt_clause: with the distinctive hyena silhouette — front shoulders noticeably taller than the rear haunches, creating a sloped back

golden-langur.label: Golden cream fur
golden-langur.prompt_clause: with golden-cream colored fur as the species' defining feature — NOT grey or black langur coloring

snow-leopard.label: Rosette spot pattern
snow-leopard.prompt_clause: with prominent dark rosette spots scattered across the body and tail rendered as folk-painted dark medallions on the smoky-grey-white base — these rosettes are the species' identifying mark and MUST be visible

snow-leopard.label: Extremely thick long tail
snow-leopard.prompt_clause: with an extraordinarily thick and long tail (nearly as long as the body) — defining trait of the snow leopard, never a thin or short tail

snow-leopard.label: Smoky grey + white fur
snow-leopard.prompt_clause: with smoky-grey-and-white body coloring (NOT yellow leopard tan, NOT orange tiger fur) — this is the snow leopard's defining palette

sarus-crane.label: Red head + upper neck
sarus-crane.prompt_clause: with bright red bare head and upper neck — the species-defining feature

indian-wild-boar.label: Both curved tusks visible
indian-wild-boar.prompt_clause: with BOTH prominent upward-curving lower tusks clearly visible on either side of the mouth — the species' identifying feature for adult males
indian-wild-boar.anti_negative: no missing tusks, no tuskless face, no juvenile-looking face

indian-giant-squirrel.label: Very long bushy tail (= body length)

nilgai.label: Blue-grey body (male) or tawny (female)
nilgai.prompt_clause: with the distinctive BLUE-GREY body color for a male nilgai (literally 'blue bull' — slate-grey hue, NOT brown deer color)

nilgai.label: Throat tassel (male)
nilgai.prompt_clause: with the male's distinctive black throat tassel/beard tuft hanging from the lower neck — a key species marker

nilgai.label: White socks on lower legs
nilgai.prompt_clause: with visible white socks/stockings on the lower legs, contrasting against the blue-grey body

nilgai.label: Horse-like long-legged build
nilgai.prompt_clause: with the species' tall horse-like long-legged build — taller and leggier than common deer, with sloped back

indian-fox.label: Small slender size (NOT red fox bulk)

great-indian-hornbill.label: Yellow curved casque on top of beak
great-indian-hornbill.prompt_clause: with the iconic yellow curved casque sitting on top of the large beak — the species' defining feature

cobra.label: EXACTLY ONE forked tongue (not two)
cobra.prompt_clause: with EXACTLY ONE single forked tongue extending from the mouth — there is ONE tongue, not two; the tongue is a single Y-shape (forked at the tip), NEVER two parallel tongues, NEVER duplicated tongues, NEVER any hallucinated extra tongue. The cobra has ONE mouth and ONE tongue. This is non-negotiable — the rendered image must show exactly one tongue.
cobra.anti_negative: no two tongues, no double tongue, no parallel tongues, no hallucinated extra tongue, no tongue duplicates

cobra.label: Extended hood (spread)
cobra.prompt_clause: with the iconic spread hood extended behind the head — the species' defining display posture

whale-shark.label: White spots + stripes on dark blue
